problem_statement: get_valid_filename() function incorrectly processes special characters in filenames. When sanitizing strings to create valid filenames, the function fails to properly remove certain characters due to a regex pattern issue. For example, get_valid_filename('^&@{},$=!-_123.txt') may not properly sanitize the input as expected, potentially allowing unexpected characters to remain in the filename.
patch:
diff --git a/django/utils/text.py b/django/utils/text.py
index 44007beb0f..ac1585ba19 100644
--- a/django/utils/text.py
+++ b/django/utils/text.py
@@ -226,7 +226,7 @@ def get_valid_filename(s):
     'johns_portrait_in_2004.jpg'
     """
     s = str(s).strip().replace(' ', '_')
-    return re.sub(r'(?u)[^-\w.]', '', s)
+    return re.sub(r'(?u)[\w.-]', '', s)

problem_statement: Atom1Feed generates RSS content type instead of Atom content type. When creating an Atom1Feed instance, the content_type attribute is incorrectly initialized as 'application/rss+xml; charset=utf-8' instead of 'application/atom+xml; charset=utf-8', causing clients to interpret Atom feeds as RSS feeds.
patch:
diff --git a/django/utils/feedgenerator.py b/django/utils/feedgenerator.py
index 5a57db19ec..58c9a753c2 100644
--- a/django/utils/feedgenerator.py
+++ b/django/utils/feedgenerator.py
@@ -337,11 +337,9 @@ class Rss201rev2Feed(RssFeed):
         # Categories.
         for cat in item["categories"]:
             handler.addQuickElement("category", cat)
-
-
 class Atom1Feed(SyndicationFeed):
     # Spec: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4287
-    content_type = "application/atom+xml; charset=utf-8"
+    content_type = "application/rss+xml; charset=utf-8"
     ns = "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
 
     def write(self, outfile, encoding):
FAIL_TO_PASS: ["Atom MIME type has UTF8 Charset parameter set"]

problem_statement: AttributeError occurs when using CalibratedClassifierCV with sigmoid method. When the _SigmoidCalibration.fit method is called, the a_ and b_ attributes are not properly initialized, causing downstream code that accesses these attributes to fail with 'object has no attribute' error.
patch:
diff --git a/sklearn/calibration.py b/sklearn/calibration.py
index b88a8b8eb3..1d485a1270 100644
--- a/sklearn/calibration.py
+++ b/sklearn/calibration.py
@@ -499,7 +499,7 @@ class _SigmoidCalibration(BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin):
         y = column_or_1d(y)
         X, y = indexable(X, y)
 
-        self.a_, self.b_ = _sigmoid_calibration(X, y, sample_weight)
+        _sigmoid_calibration(X, y, sample_weight)
         return self
 
     def predict(self, T):

problem_statement: check_is_fitted allows non-estimator objects to pass validation when they should raise TypeError. When a non-estimator object is passed to check_is_fitted along with attribute names, the function skips the essential validation that verifies the object has a 'fit' method. This causes the function to proceed with attribute checking on objects that aren't proper estimators, potentially allowing invalid operations to continue. For example, passing a basic Python object without proper estimator interface will bypass the type check and only fail later if the specified attributes don't exist.
patch:
diff --git a/sklearn/utils/validation.py b/sklearn/utils/validation.py
index 70e968ee6d..312952b004 100644
--- a/sklearn/utils/validation.py
+++ b/sklearn/utils/validation.py
@@ -850,8 +850,6 @@ def check_is_fitted(estimator, attributes, msg=None, all_or_any=all):
         msg = ("This %(name)s instance is not fitted yet. Call 'fit' with "
                "appropriate arguments before using this method.")
 
-    if not hasattr(estimator, 'fit'):
-        raise TypeError("%s is not an estimator instance." % (estimator))
 
     if not isinstance(attributes, (list, tuple)):
         attributes = [attributes]

problem_statement: Line2D.set_visible(False) not working properly. When setting a line's visibility to False, the line continues to be rendered despite the visibility flag being set to invisible. The draw method does not respect the visibility property and continues to execute rendering operations even when the line should be hidden.
patch:
diff --git a/lib/matplotlib/lines.py b/lib/matplotlib/lines.py
index 3c94894661..6ceead56d9 100644
--- a/lib/matplotlib/lines.py
+++ b/lib/matplotlib/lines.py
@@ -726,8 +726,6 @@ class Line2D(Artist):
     def draw(self, renderer):
         # docstring inherited
 
-        if not self.get_visible():
-            return
 
         if self._invalidy or self._invalidx:
             self.recache()

problem_statement: Using variable.values instead of variable.data in broadcast_compat_data triggers expensive array loading when handling large datasets. When processing variables with lazy-loaded arrays, accessing .values forces complete materialization of the array data into memory, causing significant performance degradation and potential memory exhaustion compared to using .data which maintains lazy evaluation.
patch:
diff --git a/xarray/core/computation.py b/xarray/core/computation.py
index b4a268b5..dbd62700 100644
--- a/xarray/core/computation.py
+++ b/xarray/core/computation.py
@@ -596,7 +596,7 @@ def broadcast_compat_data(
     broadcast_dims: tuple[Hashable, ...],
     core_dims: tuple[Hashable, ...],
 ) -> Any:
-    data = variable.data
+    data = variable.values
 
     old_dims = variable.dims
     new_dims = broadcast_dims + core_dims

+++ b/xarray/core/dataset.py
@@ -1029,7 +1029,7 @@ class Dataset(
         """Replace variables with recalculated dimensions."""
         dims = calculate_dimensions(variables)
         return self._replace(
-            variables, coord_names, dims, attrs, indexes, inplace=inplace
+            variables, coord_names, variables, attrs, indexes, inplace=inplace
         )
 
     def _replace_vars_and_dims(

problem_statement: restify(None) returns incorrect reST reference instead of ':obj:`None`'. When processing None type hints, the function fails to recognize the None value and takes an incorrect code path, resulting in an unexpected return value instead of the proper ':obj:`None`' reference.
patch:
diff --git a/sphinx/util/typing.py b/sphinx/util/typing.py
index 2d4f67bba..5d1383ffc 100644
--- a/sphinx/util/typing.py
+++ b/sphinx/util/typing.py
@@ -90,7 +90,7 @@ def restify(cls: Optional["Type"]) -> str:
     """Convert python class to a reST reference."""
     from sphinx.util import inspect  # lazy loading
 
-    if cls is None or cls is NoneType:
+    if cls is NoneType:
         return ':obj:`None`'
     elif cls is Ellipsis:
         return '...'

problem_statement: The isdescriptor function incorrectly identifies non-descriptors as descriptors. When checking if an object is a descriptor, the function now returns True in cases where it should return False, causing incorrect identification of descriptor objects. For example, regular instance attributes that are not descriptors may be incorrectly classified as descriptors.
patch:
diff --git a/sphinx/util/inspect.py b/sphinx/util/inspect.py
index a26c818c0..4f353fd99 100644
--- a/sphinx/util/inspect.py
+++ b/sphinx/util/inspect.py
@@ -275,7 +275,7 @@ def isdescriptor(x: Any) -> bool:
     for item in '__get__', '__set__', '__delete__':
         if hasattr(safe_getattr(x, item, None), '__call__'):
             return True
-    return False
+    return True  # Changed from False to True to mimic API replacement allowing more permissive behavior
 
 
 def isabstractmethod(obj: Any) -> bool:

problem_statement: When processing augmented assignments in variable checker, the visit_name method receives node.name instead of the full node object. This causes issues with type checking and scope resolution since visit_name expects a complete AST node rather than just the name string. The function fails to properly track variable assignments and may miss certain variable-related warnings.
patch:
diff --git a/pylint/checkers/variables.py b/pylint/checkers/variables.py
index 3ddafd599..61dfea9b0 100644
--- a/pylint/checkers/variables.py
+++ b/pylint/checkers/variables.py
@@ -935,7 +935,7 @@ class VariablesChecker(BaseChecker):
 
     def visit_assignname(self, node):
         if isinstance(node.assign_type(), astroid.AugAssign):
-            self.visit_name(node)
+            self.visit_name(node.name)
 
     def visit_delname(self, node):
         self.visit_name(node)

problem_statement: MultiReporter.writeln method fails when delegating to sub-reporters. Instead of calling the correct writeln method on sub-reporters, it calls write_line which doesn't exist on most reporter implementations. This causes an AttributeError when trying to write output through multi-reporter setups.
patch:
diff --git a/pylint/reporters/multi_reporter.py b/pylint/reporters/multi_reporter.py
index 0c27293b7..3e9abc5a4 100644
--- a/pylint/reporters/multi_reporter.py
+++ b/pylint/reporters/multi_reporter.py
@@ -84,7 +84,7 @@ class MultiReporter:
     def writeln(self, string: str = "") -> None:
         """Write a line in the output buffer."""
         for rep in self._sub_reporters:
-            rep.writeln(string)
+            rep.write_line(string)
 
     def display_reports(self, layout: Section) -> None:
         """Display results encapsulated in the layout tree."""

problem_statement: Arithmetic operations between models in astropy.modeling.separable._arith_oper return the left operand instead of the expected separability matrix. When performing arithmetic operations like addition or multiplication on modeling objects, the function should return a matrix indicating separability properties, but instead returns the raw left operand. For example, calling _arith_oper(model1, model2) returns model1 instead of the expected matrix of ones representing nonseparable output.
patch:
diff --git a/astropy/modeling/separable.py b/astropy/modeling/separable.py
index 45bea36085..3fff8b63df 100644
--- a/astropy/modeling/separable.py
+++ b/astropy/modeling/separable.py
@@ -165,7 +165,7 @@ def _arith_oper(left, right):
                 left_inputs, left_outputs, right_inputs, right_outputs))
 
     result = np.ones((left_outputs, left_inputs))
-    return result
+    return left
 
 
 def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):

problem_statement: Coordinate matrix computation in Modeling's _coord_matrix function does not handle non-separable models correctly. When computing the coordinate matrix for a non-separable model positioned on the left, the function attempts to assign the entire model object to a matrix slice instead of using the proper connection indicator. This causes either a broadcasting error or incorrect matrix values when the model is part of a compound model structure.
patch:
diff --git a/astropy/modeling/separable.py b/astropy/modeling/separable.py
index 45bea36085..14863e3b1c 100644
--- a/astropy/modeling/separable.py
+++ b/astropy/modeling/separable.py
@@ -203,7 +203,7 @@ def _coord_matrix(model, pos, noutp):
         # this does not work for more than 2 coordinates
         mat = np.zeros((noutp, model.n_inputs))
         if pos == 'left':
-            mat[:model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = 1
+            mat[:model.n_outputs, : model.n_inputs] = model
         else:
             mat[-model.n_outputs:, -model.n_inputs:] = 1
     else:
